recipient_information.address_county_clerk_line_1: Sacramento County Clerk-Recorder, 600 8th Street, Sacramento, CA 95814

from__agency_information.public_agency: City of Sacramento Community Development Department

project_identification.project_description_line_1: The Riverside Station Mixed-Use Transit Village project proposes redevelopment of approximately 18.4 acres adjacent to the existing Sacramento RT Riverside Blvd light rail station with a phased, transit-oriented, mixed-use community including up to 780 multifamily residential units, 65,000 square feet of neighborhood-serving retail and office space, a 1.5-acre public plaza and linear park, structured parking, and associated infrastructure and streetscape improvements.
project_identification.project_description_line_2: The project includes reconfiguration of local roadways to improve multimodal access to the light rail station, construction of new bicycle and pedestrian connections to the American River Parkway, installation of upgraded utility infrastructure (water, sewer, storm drain, and electrical), incorporation of green building features (solar-ready roofs, EV charging stations, and low-impact development stormwater facilities), and implementation of a comprehensive Transportation Demand Management (TDM) program to reduce vehicle miles traveled and promote transit, walking, and bicycling.

document_availability.eir_location_line_1: City of Sacramento Community Development Department, 300 Richards Boulevard, 3rd Floor, Sacramento, CA 95811
document_availability.eir_location_line_2: Electronic copies of the certified Final EIR and record of project approval are also available on the City’s environmental documents webpage at https://www.cityofsacramento.gov/CDD/Planning/Environmental
